title: Use diversity-first OOF ensembling with post-hoc calibration
summary: AUC often improves from combining differently biased tabular models, and scikit-learn’s stacking/calibration APIs are designed around cross-validated predictions.
rationale: AUC often improves from combining differently biased tabular models, and scikit-learn’s stacking/calibration APIs are designed around cross-validated predictions. CatBoost native-categorical models, histogram GBDTs, and query-rank models should make different errors; calibrated blending can preserve ranking while fixing over/under-confidence.
implementation_hint: Train 2-4 diverse OOF bases: current classifier, CatBoost native-cat model, ranking model, and a constrained HistGradientBoosting model. Blend by rank-average or a logistic `StackingClassifier`, then calibrate final scores with OOF sigmoid or temperature scaling; compare to raw blend because AUC sometimes prefers uncalibrated ranks.
expected_effect: Often yields a modest but reliable bump when single-model feature work is saturating near a CV ceiling.
risk: Stacking can overfit if OOF discipline is broken; isotonic may overfit if calibration folds are too small or regime-shifted.
